chunk_summary: This section outlines decision nodes for outpatient follow-up of children with moderate acute malnutrition (MAM), focusing on Day 0, weekly, and monthly assessments to monitor progress, identify deterioration, and escalate care if needed.
learning_objectives: Understand the decision-making process for outpatient follow-up of MAM cases, including Day 0, weekly, and monthly nodes.
Identify high-risk children based on WHO prioritization factors and schedule appropriate follow-ups.
Learn to assess adherence, anthropometry, and clinical signs to determine escalation or continuation of care.
Recognize failure thresholds and initiate investigations or referrals for non-responding cases.
key_facts: Day 0: Confirm MUAC (115–119 mm for high-risk), WHZ/WLZ, oedema, appetite, and danger signs; classify high-risk cases and schedule review in 7 days.
Day 7/14: Repeat anthropometry, verify adherence, and escalate care if weight loss, falling MUAC, or clinical deterioration is observed.
Week 4: If no response despite adherence, initiate illness screening (e.g., persistent diarrhoea, UTI, TB, anemia) and consider referral.
Week 8: Failure threshold; investigate and refer if comorbidities or safeguarding concerns persist.
High-risk factors include MUAC 115–119 mm, WAZ < -3, age < 24 months, relapse, prior SAM, comorbidities, or severe social adversity.
reference_quote: Classify as high-risk when any WHO prioritization factor is present (MUAC 115–119 mm, WAZ < -3, age under 24 months, relapse, prior SAM, comorbidity or disability, or severe social adversity) and schedule review in 7 days.
